title: Government-Paid Maternity Leave (GPML)
text:
Government-Paid Maternity Leave (GPML) - SupportGoWhere
Government-Paid Maternity Leave (GPML)
Share link
Provides up to 16 weeks of paid leave for eligible working mothers to care for their newborns.
SCHEME HIGHLIGHTS
Up to 16 weeks of Government-Paid Maternity Leave (GPML) for eligible working mothers
For the first and second child, the employer pays for the first 8 weeks, and the government reimburses the 9th to 16th week, capped at $10,000 per 4 weeks, up to $20,000
For the third and subsequent child, the government reimburses all 16 weeks, capped at $10,000 per 4 weeks, up to $40,000
Apply now
Collapse all sections
About this support

Up to 16 weeks of Government-Paid Maternity Leave (GPML) for eligible working mothers.

For the first and second child, the employer pays for the first 8 weeks, and the government reimburses the 9th to 16th week, capped at $10,000 per 4 weeks, up to $20,000 per child order.

For the third and subsequent child, the government reimburses all 16 weeks, capped at $10,000 per 4 weeks, up to $40,000 per child order.

Option to share up to 4 weeks of GPML with the spouse under the Government-Paid Shared Parental Leave scheme.

Who is this for?

For live births:

Singapore Citizen or Permanent Resident

Child is a Singapore Citizen at birth or within 12 months after birth

For employees: Served employer for at least 3 months before child's birth (GPML that is granted despite not meeting this criterion cannot be shared with your husband under the SPL scheme)

For self-employed: Engaged in work for at least 3 months before child's birth and lost income during maternity leave

For stillbirths: 
Similar conditions apply, with additional criteria:

The date of stillbirth is on or after 1 November 2021

You are a Singapore Citizen at the time of stillbirth or the child’s father is a Singapore Citizen and was lawfully married to the mother at some point between conception and stillbirth

How to apply?

For employees:

Notify your employer at least one week before starting maternity leave.

Submit the declaration form (GPML1) to your employer with necessary documents at least one week before starting your Government-Paid Maternity Leave (GPML), or within one month from your child’s birth.

Follow your employer’s leave application process.

For self-employed:

Keep a record of your GPML dates

Submit your claim online via the Government-Paid Leave (GPL) Portal after starting the government-paid portion of your GPML

Claims should be made within three months after the last day of your GPML

For employers:

Obtain and verify the declaration form (GPML1) from your employee

Keep the declaration form or records for five years from the last GPML date

Submit your claim for reimbursement online via the GPL Portal after your employee has taken the government-paid portion of GPML

Claims should be made within three months after the last day of the employee’s GPML

Where can I find help?

View more details

MSFCares: 1800 111 2222 
(say ‘GPLS’ when asked for the hotline you want to reach)

Airtime charges apply for mobile calls to 1800 service lines. Calls are free of charge only if made from regular land lines in Singapore.

Submit your enquiry

Scheme last updated 15 Dec 2025
SUPPORT RECOMMENDER
Answer a few questions to find schemes and services you may be eligible for.
